input.title: Event Management Platform
input.description: Event Management Platform is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Python Django, PostgreSQL, Celery for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Event Creation
requirements: Create events with title, description, date, time, and venue
Support multiple event types (conference, workshop, concert, webinar)
Upload event banner and promotional images
Set ticket types and pricing tiers

FR-02.title: Ticket Booking
requirements: Browse and search events
Select ticket type and quantity
Secure payment processing
E-ticket with QR code generation

FR-03.title: Venue Management
requirements: Add venues with capacity, address, and floor plan
Room/hall configuration within venue
Seat map creation for assigned seating
Venue availability calendar

FR-04.title: Speaker and Agenda Management
requirements: Add speakers with bio, photo, and topic
Create event agenda with time slots
Assign speakers to sessions
Attendee session bookmarks

FR-05.title: Attendee Check-in
requirements: QR code scanning at venue entrance
Self-check-in kiosks
Real-time attendance tracking
Badge printing on check-in

FR-06.title: Event Analytics
requirements: Ticket sales reports and revenue tracking
Attendee demographics analysis
Session popularity metrics
Post-event survey results

FR-07.title: Sponsorship Management
requirements: Create sponsor tiers (platinum, gold, silver)
Sponsor profile pages with logo and description
Sponsor visibility on event page
Sponsorship package pricing

FR-08.title: Email Campaign Management
requirements: Send invitation emails to mailing lists
Automated reminder emails before event
Post-event thank you and survey emails
Email analytics (open rate, click rate)

FR-09.title: Virtual Event Support
requirements: Live streaming integration for virtual events
Virtual networking rooms
Screen sharing for presentations
Virtual booth for sponsors

FR-10.title: Refund and Cancellation
requirements: Attendee can request refund per policy
Event cancellation by organizer with full refund
Partial refund options
Refund processing within 7 business days

FR-01.title: Design Event Creation UI
FR-01.description: Design the user interface for event creation. Create the screens, forms, dashboards, or status views needed by the SRS and make the workflow clear, responsive, and accessible for the target users.
acceptance_criteria: UI design for event creation is complete and reviewable.
The event creation design is responsive and accessible.
The design for event creation aligns with the documented user flow and validation states.

FR-01.title: Frontend Implementation of Event Creation
FR-01.description: Implement the frontend behavior for event creation. Build the required user interactions, validation states, loading states, and error handling so the interface supports these behaviors: Create events with title, description, date, time, and venue; Support multiple event types (conference, workshop, concert, webinar); Upload event banner and promotional images.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend implements the required event creation user interactions.
Validation, loading, and error states for event creation are handled in the UI.
Frontend behavior for event creation is integrated correctly with backend APIs.

FR-01.title: Backend Implementation of Event Creation
FR-01.description: Implement the backend logic for event creation. Build the required APIs, business rules, validation, permissions, persistence, and processing needed to support these behaviors: Create events with title, description, date, time, and venue; Support multiple event types (conference, workshop, concert, webinar); Upload event banner and promotional images.
acceptance_criteria: Backend logic for event creation is implemented and testable.
APIs and business rules for event creation enforce validation and security requirements.
Data handling for event creation is reliable and supports the required workflow.

FR-01.title: Database Design for Event Creation
FR-01.description: Design the data model and persistence strategy for event creation. Define entities, relationships, indexes, retention rules, and migration changes needed to store and query the workflow reliably.
acceptance_criteria: The data model for event creation supports the required workflow.
Relationships, constraints, and indexes for event creation are defined correctly.
Schema changes for event creation can be applied safely in target environments.

FR-01.title: Testing Event Creation Functionality
FR-01.description: Create and execute tests for event creation. Cover the main success flows, failure scenarios, permission checks, validation rules, integrations, and operational edge cases described in the SRS so the implementation is safe to release.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover the main success and failure scenarios for event creation.
Security, validation, and permission checks for event creation are verified.
Regression coverage exists for the most important event creation behaviors.
